summary: George Hotz critiques the newly announced University of Austin, arguing that despite its marketing as a bastion of free thought against mainstream academia, it's actually a spin-off of the existing elite rather than a genuine counter-movement. He points out that its founders (Lonsdale, Altman, Andreessen) are successful products of the current system, and its faculty are merely 'lightly cancelled' establishment figures. Hotz argues that true disruption of universities requires building something 10x better with a positive vision, not just opposing the existing system. He concludes that any serious alternative university project would need to build an accompanying 'religion' or compelling narrative to succeed.
key_points: The University of Austin's founders and faculty are establishment figures rebranding, not a genuine counter-elite
NGO awards and institutional credentials are 'fake status signaling markers' that reveal the project's true nature
Modern Silicon Valley ideology is 'grotesque' - treating people as data to be optimized and integrated
Real disruption comes from building something 10x better, not defining yourself in opposition to existing institutions
A successful alternative university would require building a 'religion' - a compelling narrative that answers fundamental questions about meaning and society
money_quotes: This is not a counter elite! This is a spin off of the exact same bs that's everywhere.
Modern Silicon Valleyism is a grotesque ideology formed by psychopaths.
Fuck you, I'm not a fucking piece of data. I will not be optimized, integrated, or transformed.
If people don't talk in normal words, that's a huge red flag.
You don't do it by defining yourself in opposition to the existing, you just build something 10x better.
If someone is serious about the university project, I don't believe it's doable without also building a religion.
The University of Austin will have a Diversity and Inclusion department in 10 years. But don't worry, they stand against whatever new wacko left thing there is in 2030.
key_insight: Genuine institutional disruption requires a positive vision and compelling narrative for the future, not establishment figures rebranding their opposition to current trends as revolutionary change.
